result:
I would give every person the ability to feel, for one unshielded moment, the sum total of everyone else’s pain as if it were their own.

Not so people would collapse under the weight of it, but so they could never again pretend that the stranger’s wound isn’t also their own. Borders, grudges, and petty cruelties would evaporate the instant the fragility behind every pair of eyes became unbearably obvious.

It would be a brutal gift—maybe only a minute of perfect empathy—but afterward no one could un-know what they’d felt, and the calculus of indifference would become impossible.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
